description: ElementStore system class definitions — the meta-schema that defines how ElementStore works

classes.name: Class
classes.description: Defines a data class with its properties and behaviors

props.label: Name
props.description: Display name of the class

props.label: Description
props.description: What this class represents

props.label: Extends
props.description: Parent class for inheritance

props.label: Properties
props.description: Property definitions for this class

props.label: Table Name
props.description: Custom table/collection name for storage

props.label: Storage
props.description: Storage provider (falls back to parent class, then default)

props.label: System Class
props.description: Protected system class (cannot be deleted)

props.label: Abstract
props.description: Cannot create instances directly (only via child classes)

props.label: Providers
props.description: Data providers for external API integration

props.label: External Links
props.description: Maps storage_id to external ID for provider-linked objects. Managed by ActionExecutor — never set manually.

props.label: Genesis File
props.description: Source genesis/seed file in .es/ directory. Set automatically by GenesisLoader for seed write-back.

classes.name: Property
classes.description: Defines a property within a class — type, validation, and UI behavior

props.label: Data Type
props.description: Type of value this property holds

props.label: Is Array
props.description: Property holds multiple values

props.label: Key
props.description: Property key (field name in data)

props.label: Label
props.description: Display label in UI

props.label: Description
props.description: Help text shown to users

props.label: Type Options
props.description: Type-specific options (varies by data_type)

props.label: Target Classes
props.description: Class IDs for relations/embedded objects

props.label: Strict Class
props.description: Only accept exact class, not child classes

props.label: On Orphan
props.description: Action when referenced object is deleted

props.label: Editor
props.description: Embedded @editor config — object picked from @editor instances filtered by data_type match

props.label: Field Type
props.description: Shorthand field type string (e.g. email, url, textarea)

props.label: Display Order
props.description: Sort order in forms (lower = first)

props.label: Group
props.description: Form section grouping

props.label: Hidden
props.description: Hide from default UI views

props.label: Required
props.description: Field must have a value

props.label: Read Only
props.description: Field cannot be edited after creation

props.label: Create Only
props.description: Only writable when creating new objects

props.label: Default Value
props.description: Default value for new objects

props.label: Validators
props.description: Validation rules — relation to @function instances

props.label: Server Only
props.description: Stripped from API responses (backend-only)

props.label: Master Only
props.description: Only visible on master/admin interface

classes.name: Editor
classes.description: Defines a UI editor component for editing property values. Instances are seed data (text, textarea, select, etc.)

props.label: Name
props.description: Editor display name

props.label: Description
props.description: What this editor is for

props.label: Data Types
props.description: Supported data types

props.label: Default
props.description: Default editor for its data types

props.label: System Editor
props.description: Protected system editor (cannot be deleted)

props.label: Validator
props.description: Built-in validator (e.g. email, url, phone)

props.label: Options Schema
props.description: Configuration options this editor accepts

props.label: Component
props.description: UI component name or path

props.label: Render
props.description: Custom render function: (scope) => html

classes.name: Storage
classes.description: Storage provider configuration — defines where and how class data is persisted. 'api' and 'composite' types proxy through @provider.

props.label: Name
props.description: Storage provider display name

props.label: URL
props.description: API endpoint URL (for rest type)

props.label: Type
props.description: Storage backend: local (browser), rest (ES API), api (external via @provider), seed (read-only), composite (multi-source), couchdb/mysql/json (server-only)

props.label: Provider
props.description: Data provider for external API integration (type=api)

props.label: Read Sources
props.description: Ordered storage IDs to read from (type=composite). Tried in order or merged.

props.label: Write Target
props.description: Storage ID to write to (type=composite).

props.label: Read Strategy
props.description: How to combine reads (type=composite): fallback=first hit wins, merge=combine all

props.label: Write Strategy
props.description: How to handle writes (type=composite): sequential=in order stop on fail, parallel=all at once, best_effort=all ignore failures

classes.name: Function
classes.description: Defines a reusable function — validators, transformers, generators, or computed fields

props.label: Name
props.description: Function display name

props.label: Description
props.description: What this function does

props.label: Function Type
props.description: Category of function

props.label: Scope
props.description: Which data types this function applies to

props.label: Parameters
props.description: Function input parameters schema

props.label: Code
props.description: JavaScript: (obj, prop, value, params) => result

props.label: System
props.description: Protected system function

classes.name: Action
classes.description: Universal execution unit — a named function with typed parameters and field mapping. The action key (id) is the function name. The type determines HOW it executes: api (HTTP call), cli (shell command), function (registry lookup), event (bus dispatch), composite (chain), ui (JS handler). Parameters map between the source object and the request/response via request_mapping and response_mapping.

props.label: Name
props.description: Action display name

props.label: Description
props.description: What this action does

props.label: Type
props.description: Execution type: api (HTTP), cli (shell), function (registry), event (bus), composite (chain), ui (JS handler)

props.label: Group
props.description: Action category for UI grouping

props.label: Target Class
props.description: Class this action applies to. Determines which objects can invoke this action.

props.label: Parameters
props.description: Input parameters schema — defines what the action accepts

props.label: Returns
props.description: Return type

props.label: Request Mapping
props.description: Source object field → request field mapping. Maps current object properties to action input parameters. e.g. {application_id: "app", environment_id: "env"}

props.label: Response Mapping
props.description: Response field → target object field mapping. Maps action output back to object properties. e.g. {status: "last_status", updated: "last_deploy_at"}

props.label: Provider
props.description: Provider that owns this action. Inherits base_url and auth from provider (type=api). Provider.actions[] is the canonical list.

props.label: HTTP Method
props.description: HTTP method (type=api)

props.label: Endpoint
props.description: URL path relative to provider base_url. Supports {field} placeholders from source object (type=api)

props.label: Headers
props.description: Additional HTTP headers as key:value object (type=api)

props.label: Command
props.description: Shell command template with {field} placeholders from source object (type=cli)

props.label: Working Directory
props.description: Directory to run command from. Supports {field} placeholders (type=cli)

props.label: Function Key
props.description: FunctionRegistry key to call (type=function), e.g. 'billing.calculate'

props.label: Event Name
props.description: EventBus event name to emit (type=event)

props.label: Payload Map
props.description: param→event_field mapping for event payload (type=event)

props.label: Sub-Actions
props.description: Ordered action IDs to chain (type=composite)

props.label: Strategy
props.description: Execution strategy (type=composite): sequential=stop on fail, parallel=all at once

props.label: Handler
props.description: JS handler code: (scope) => result (type=ui)

props.label: Requires Selection
props.description: Action requires selected object(s) (type=ui)

props.label: Bulk Action
props.description: Can apply to multiple objects (type=ui)

props.label: Confirm
props.description: Confirmation message before running (empty = no confirm)

props.label: Icon
props.description: Icon name for UI display

classes.name: Event
classes.description: Defines an event that can be subscribed to and triggered

props.label: Name
props.description: Event name

props.label: Description
props.description: What triggers this event

props.label: Target Class
props.description: Class this event applies to

props.label: Trigger
props.description: When this event fires

props.label: Handler
props.description: Event handler: (scope) => void

props.label: Payload Schema
props.description: Event payload structure

props.label: Async
props.description: Run handler asynchronously

props.label: Priority
props.description: Execution order (higher = first)

classes.name: Provider
classes.description: Abstract base for data providers — defines how to connect to an external API. Extend this class to create concrete providers per external system. Use extends_id to inherit base_url and auth across multiple class-specific providers.

props.label: Name
props.description: Provider display name

props.label: Description
props.description: What external system this provider connects to

props.label: Base URL
props.description: Base URL for API requests (inherited by child providers via extends_id)

props.label: Authentication
props.description: Auth config: {type: bearer|basic|apikey, token?, username?, password?, header?, key?}

props.label: External ID Field
props.description: Field name in API response that holds the external ID (stored in object._links)

props.label: Write Mode
props.description: crud=direct setObject allowed; actions_only=must use @action to modify linked objects

props.label: Field Mapping
props.description: Default API field → ES field mapping (overridden per action). e.g. {api_id: id, api_name: name}

props.label: Actions
props.description: Available actions on this provider (get_one, get_list, create_one, update_one, delete_one, + custom)

props.label: Default Parameters
props.description: Default query parameters added to every API request

classes.name: CRUD Provider
classes.description: Concrete provider template for standard REST CRUD APIs. Inherits base_url and auth from @provider. Adds URL patterns and pagination config. Create instances for each class that uses a CRUD API.

props.label: Get One Endpoint
props.description: Endpoint path for GET single object, e.g. /vms/{id}. Overrides actions.get_one.endpoint

props.label: Get List Endpoint
props.description: Endpoint path for GET list, e.g. /vms

props.label: Create One Endpoint
props.description: Endpoint path for POST create, e.g. /vms

props.label: Update One Endpoint
props.description: Endpoint path for PUT/PATCH update, e.g. /vms/{id}

props.label: Delete One Endpoint
props.description: Endpoint path for DELETE, e.g. /vms/{id}

props.label: Paginator
props.description: Pagination config: {page_param, size_param, total_field, data_field}

props.label: Filter Params
props.description: Available filter query parameter names

classes.name: Auth Config
classes.description: Auth-service connection configuration for this elementStore installation. One object per installation. All endpoint paths are configurable here.

props.label: App ID
props.description: Identifier for this app in the auth-service (e.g. 'elementStore')

props.label: App Name
props.description: Human-readable display name registered in auth-service

props.label: Auth Service URL
props.description: Base URL of the auth-service (e.g. http://agura-auth:3100)

props.label: Enabled
props.description: Enable auth enforcement on all API requests

props.label: Scopes
props.description: Permission scopes this app requests (e.g. read, write, admin)

props.label: Allowed Origins
props.description: Browser origins allowed to use tokens issued for this app

props.label: Login Endpoint
props.description: POST — authenticate user, returns JWT

props.label: Logout Endpoint
props.description: POST — invalidate token

props.label: Verify Endpoint
props.description: GET — verify a token (remote check, optional)

props.label: Refresh Endpoint
props.description: POST — refresh an expiring JWT

props.label: JWKS Endpoint
props.description: GET — fetch public keys for local JWT verification

props.label: Register App Endpoint
props.description: POST — register this app with auth-service on first boot

props.label: Get App Endpoint
props.description: GET — check if app is already registered (appended with /{app_id})

props.label: Register Machine
props.description: POST — register this machine/instance (appended with /{app_id}/machines)

props.label: List Machines
props.description: GET — list registered machines for this app

props.label: Revoke Machine
props.description: DELETE — revoke a specific machine registration

props.label: Public Key (cached)
props.description: Cached JWT public key fetched from JWKS endpoint at boot

props.label: Key ID (kid)
props.description: Key identifier from JWKS — used to select key for verification

props.label: Key Fetched At
props.description: When the public key was last fetched from auth-service

classes.name: Auth App Registration
classes.description: Credentials returned after registering this app with auth-service. One object per app_id. client_secret is server_only.

props.label: Auth Config
props.description: Auth config this registration belongs to

props.label: Client ID
props.description: Client ID assigned by auth-service after registration

props.label: Client Secret
props.description: Client secret — never exposed via API

props.label: Registered At
props.description: When this app was first registered

props.label: Active
props.description: Whether this registration is currently valid

classes.name: Seed
classes.description: Declarative data loading definition — classes to create and storage descriptors to load data from. Objects without class_id at root level are assumed to be @seed.

props.label: Name
props.description: Seed definition name

props.label: Description
props.description: What this seed definition provides

props.label: Version
props.description: Seed format version

props.label: Classes
props.description: Class definitions to create or update

props.label: Seed Sources
props.description: Storage descriptors — files or URLs to load data from

props.label: Inline Objects
props.description: Objects to create directly (optional — prefer seed sources for large datasets)

classes.name: Auth Machine
classes.description: Per-instance machine registration with auth-service. One object created/updated on each elementStore boot.

props.label: Auth Config
props.description: Auth config this machine belongs to

props.label: Instance ID
props.description: UUID generated once for this machine, persists across reboots

props.label: Machine ID
props.description: Machine ID returned by auth-service after registration

props.label: Hostname
props.description: Hostname of this machine at time of registration

props.label: IP Address
props.description: IP address of this machine at time of registration

props.label: ES Version
props.description: ElementStore version running on this machine

props.label: Registered At
props.description: When this machine was first registered

props.label: Last Boot At
props.description: Timestamp of the last successful boot registration

props.label: Active
props.description: Whether this machine is currently authorized
